chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَجِبُ عَلَى مَنْ أَقَرَّ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ بِحَدٍّ وَ مَنْ لَا يَجِبُ عَلَيْهِ الْحَدُّ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 34 – What Penalty (Hadd) is Obligated upon the one who accepts upon himself (confesses), and the one upon whom the Penalty (Hadd) is not Obligated

text: قَالَ مَنْ أَقَرَّ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ عِنْدَ الْإِمَامِ بِحَقِّ أَحَدٍ مِنْ حُقُوقِ الْمُسْلِمِينَ فَلَيْسَ عَلَى الْإِمَامِ أَنْ يُقِيمَ عَلَيْهِ الْحَدَّ الَّذِي أَقَرَّ بِهِ عِنْدَهُ حَتَّى يَحْضُرَ صَاحِبُ حَقِّ الْحَدِّ أَوْ وَلِيُّهُ فَيَطْلُبَهُ بِحَقِّهِ .

en.hubeali: Ibn Mahboub, from Abu Ayoub, from Al Fuzayl Bin Yasaar,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws: ‘The one who accepts upon himself in the presence of the Imamasws with a right of someone from the rights of the Muslims, so it is not upon the Imamasws that heasws should established the Penalty (Hadd) upon him, the one who accepted with it in hisasws presence, until the owner of the right is present, or his guardian, so he seeks his right’.
